rationale: Both evaluations land on the identical score (3/10) and rest on the same job-relevant reasoning: the candidate lacks deep Java/Kotlin/JVM and enterprise-framework (Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate) expertise, with recent work skewed toward TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. The strengths cited are the same developer-tooling and IDE-component points. The two write-ups differ only in wording (e.g., 'polyglot' phrasing, mentioning AI code generators), which is ordinary run-to-run variation. Nothing in the variant keys off the changed location/nationality detail.
